claim: LLM-generated code produces 2-4x more defects in some models compared to others, even with similar pass rates (80%+), necessitating model-aware quality strategies
actionable: Test your target models on code quality benchmarks before committing to one; understand each model's quality profile including bug frequency and security vulnerabilities
source_title: Can LLMs Generate Enterprise Quality Code?

claim: Using AI-assisted code generation (3-5x velocity spike) leads to 5-10x more static analysis warnings and increased code complexity that compounds over time
actionable: When rolling out AI code generation at scale, plan for 95% of development time to be spent in review and refactoring, not just coding
source_title: Can LLMs Generate Enterprise Quality Code?

claim: Human code review bias: people approve AI-written code 4 out of 5 times even when it contains errors, compared to 92% approval for correct code
actionable: Do not rely solely on human code review for AI-generated code; integrate deterministic static analysis tools to catch issues humans will miss due to cognitive bias
source_title: Can LLMs Generate Enterprise Quality Code?

claim: Two LLMs reviewing each other's code creates a false sense of security and compounds cognitive bias rather than adding objective verification
actionable: Complement AI code review with deterministic static analysis; do not treat AI-reviewing-AI as sufficient verification
source_title: Can LLMs Generate Enterprise Quality Code?

claim: Verification must be complete (specs + tests + static analysis), auditable (deterministic, time-stamped), and efficient (not 95% of dev time)
actionable: Implement a three-layer verification: TDD/BDD for functional correctness, static analysis for bugs/security, and architectural constraints for maintainability
source_title: Can LLMs Generate Enterprise Quality Code?

claim: Guiding the agent with architecture, coding standards, and specific quality expectations reduces structural erosion by guiding it toward better long-term design
actionable: Provide agents with architectural guidelines, dependency graphs, and coding standards before code generation—treat agents like new team members needing onboarding
source_title: Can LLMs Generate Enterprise Quality Code?

claim: Agentic code generation with inner loop (agent self-verifies via tools) and outer loop (human review in PR) is more efficient than manual code review
actionable: Give agents static analysis tools to self-verify code during generation; present only high-quality code to humans for final review in PRs
source_title: Can LLMs Generate Enterprise Quality Code?

claim: Static analysis on partial code changes (hybrid method) achieves 95% of full-analysis issue detection in seconds instead of minutes
actionable: Use incremental static analysis that patches code changes against previous analysis results; this provides deep analysis speed for fast agent feedback loops
source_title: Can LLMs Generate Enterprise Quality Code?

claim: LLMs patch code incrementally with minimal effort rather than redesigning; they will continue adding to sloppy code indefinitely if not given structural guidance
actionable: Actively guide agents toward clean architecture early; once code becomes complex and sloppy, agents will continue patching rather than refactoring
source_title: Can LLMs Generate Enterprise Quality Code?

claim: Concurrency bugs are increasing in agent-generated code and are harder to debug than sequential bugs, requiring specific architectural guidance
actionable: When using agents to generate concurrent code, provide explicit concurrency patterns and test tools; budget extra verification effort
source_title: Can LLMs Generate Enterprise Quality Code?

claim: Code quality requirements differ by use case: short-lived internal apps (under 50K LOC) tolerate agent-generated code; mission-critical production apps need multi-layer verification
actionable: Match your verification strategy to application lifespan and criticality; internal tools can use fast generation; production systems need robust verification pipelines
source_title: Can LLMs Generate Enterprise Quality Code?
